title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham was arrested after driving 118 mph and crashing in New Hampshire.
The driver fled a police stop, reaching Route 101, before crashing into a retaining wall in Stratham.
The teen faced multiple charges, including felony reckless conduct and DUI, and was held after refusing bail.

title: The ‘World’s Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade’ is still New England’s nuttiest St. Pat’s fever dream
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: The 'World’s Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade' in Adamsville, Rhode Island is 89 feet long and celebrated its fifth year.
Approximately 2,000 attendees, more than half the population of Little Compton, participated in the parade, organized by Kinnane Brothers film studio.
A documentary is in production about the parade's rivalry with Hot Springs, Arkansas, whose parade measures 98 feet.

title: Massachusetts ER doctors say long wait times reflect a health system under strain
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts emergency room doctors cite bed shortages, staffing issues, and primary care gaps for long ER wait times.
Patients in Massachusetts average over three-hour ER stays, with some reporting waits as long as 30 hours.
Aging population and hospital capacity issues, worsened by COVID-19, contribute to prolonged ER patient boarding.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about heavy rain, damaging winds on Monday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Meteorologists predict heavy rainfall and possible flooding in Boston, with 1-3 inches of rain expected.
National Weather Service issues Flood Watches and High Wind Warnings, with gusts predicted to reach 65 mph.
Residents advised to prepare for potential flooding in low-lying areas as rivers are expected to rise.

title: Firefighter suffers cardiac arrest during Hyde Park blaze
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A firefighter suffered cardiac arrest while battling a warehouse fire in Hyde Park, Boston, on Sunday night.
The fire at 1 Westinghouse Plaza caused an estimated $750,000 in damages, according to Deputy Chief Brian Tully.
The cause of the fire remains under investigation by the Boston Fire Investigation Unit; no other injuries reported.

title: Mass. native’s 44-point performance helped Penn reach NCAA Tournament in dramatic fashion
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: T.J. Power scored 44 points to lead Penn over Yale, earning an NCAA Tournament bid.
Power set an Ivy League tournament record and hit a game-tying three-pointer to force overtime.
Penn will face third-seeded Illinois in the NCAA Tournament first round Thursday at 9:25 p.m.

title: Hundreds of flights delayed or cancelled at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Over 300 flights at Logan Airport were delayed or cancelled Monday due to severe East Coast weather.
Airlines like Delta, American, and United are waiving change fees and allowing itinerary changes for affected passengers.
Passengers are advised to check their flight status and can use the FlyLogan App for updates on their travel plans.

title: 11 charged after allegedly staging robberies to claim victim status on immigration applications
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Eleven Indian nationals were charged for allegedly staging robberies to support fraudulent U Visa applications, per the U.S. Attorney's Office.
Seven arrests occurred in Massachusetts, while others appeared in Kentucky, Missouri, and Ohio; defendants will face Boston federal court proceedings.
Patel, initially sentenced to prison in 2025, allegedly orchestrated robberies for clerks to falsely claim crime victim status for visas.

title: One injured in South End shooting
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A person was injured in a South End shooting on Sunday evening near Washington and East Berkeley Streets.
The victim was taken to a local hospital with non-life-threatening injuries, according to Boston Police.
Police continue to investigate the incident and have not yet made any arrests.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony is emerging as a superstar at World Baseball Classic
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony hit a crucial home run for Team USA, securing a 2-1 victory over the Dominican Republic in the WBC semifinals.
In the 2026 World Baseball Classic, Anthony's stats include a .318 average, two home runs, seven RBIs, and a 1.014 OPS.
Anthony is recognized as a rising star in baseball, earning praise for his skills, work ethic, and impact on the Red Sox and Team USA.
